channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nintendo delays Switch 2 preorders because of Trump’s tariffs
item.description: Nintendo is pushing back preorders for its upcoming Nintendo Switch 2 while it figures out the implications of President Donald Trump’s aggressive global tariffs. It’s unclear when preorders will actually begin. They were supposed to start Wednesday, ahead of a June 5 launch date.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: My dog recognizes the sounds a Waymo car makes
item.description: Most of us know the general (albeit simplified) story: Russian physiologist Ivan Pavlov used a stimulus—like a metronome—around the dogs he was studying, and soon, the hounds would start to salivate. They had learned that the sound meant food was coming.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Intel could be key to Nvidia's future plans for megawatt-class rack servers as it pushes ahead with Superfluid cooling
item.description: Intel’s Superfluid cooling solution is capable of handling 1.5kW of heat and could be used on Nvidia's GB300 superchip.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How AI is steering the media toward a ‘close enough’ standard
item.description: The nonstop cavalcade of announcements in the AI world has created a kind of reality distortion field. There is so much buzz, and even more money, circulating in the industry that it feels almost sacrilegious to doubt that AI will make good on its promises to change the world.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: Motorola’s new cheap phone offers great value for money – plus it feels like a canvas tote bag
item.description: Motorola has revealed the Edge 60 Fusion handset, which packs solid hardware into a colorful frame – with an option for a unique canvas-style finish.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: TikTok’s new owner stands to inherit 1,500 safety, privacy lawsuits
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- TikTok faces a mountain of litigation over child safety and privacy, a big risk for potential bidders vying to save the popular video app in the US.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The iPad mini could be the next Apple tablet to get an OLED display – and I think that makes perfect sense
item.description: A new rumor suggests the iPad mini could be getting an OLED display with its next generation, becoming the second iPad to get the upgrade after the iPad Pro – here's why I think that makes a lot of sense.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Increasingly dangerous scam targets kids through sextortion
item.description: The FBI issued a report that described sextortion as “a growing threat,” noting that the agency investigated roughly 1,000 cases a month for much of 2021 through early 2023.
item.dc:creator: Andre Mouchard, The Mercury News

item.title: A future Samsung Galaxy Ring could get a unique and intriguing new way of charging
item.description: A future Samsung Galaxy Ring could charge directly from a slot in the back of a future Galaxy smartphone.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge’s new release date could be May 13
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge could be just over a month away, and we're hearing more about its price too.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI-Powered Customer Service: Cobas Asset Management's Innovative Move
item.description: Cobas Asset Management has launched a new project to enhance user experience on their website and private area with the help of an AI-powered virtual assistant.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Dr Arvind Gupta: Trustworthy systems to empower India’s growing economy
item.description: Digital India Foundation, a policy think tank working in the areas of technology policy, digital inclusion, ethics of AI, supply-chain security, and governance of critical and emerging technologies. Dr.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mechanical Orchard taps gen AI for mainframe-to-cloud modernization
item.description: IBM is expected to drive AI deep into its next mainframe refresh, due this spring, in a move aimed at keeping and attracting Big Iron customers. Meanwhile, there’s no shortage of vendors vying for enterprise customers that are interested in moving off the mainframe and into the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Accelerate cloud infrastructure initiatives with Lucid Software
item.description: Cloud and development operations (devops) teams are under constant pressure to do more, faster. They have outdated systems to update and manage, new systems to build, and requests from business units who need their support. It’s a velocity problem, and IT departments often struggle to keep pace.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Boost Agile team performance with scalable and flexible workflows with Lucid Software
item.description: Imagine one of your Agile teams is six months deep into a strategic digital initiative when they realize the solution isn’t working. Maybe it isn’t meeting customer requirements, or there is insufficient visibility into the project to specify where things are going off track.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia AI events unleash ‘unprecedented’ surge for downtown San Jose
item.description: The Nvidia AI conference in downtown San Jose triggered an "unprecedented" economic boom in the city's urban core.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Opinion: The tech fantasy that powers AI is running on fumes
item.description: We were promised life-changing transformations. Instead we get a mid-level technology that helps us write emails.
item.dc:creator: Tressie McMillan Cottom, The Mercury News

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Humanoid robot stuns with perfect side-flip acrobatics
item.description: Unitree, a Chinese robotics company that developed a backflipping robot, has now introduced a humanoid robot capable of doing a side flip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An AI watchdog accused OpenAI of using copyrighted books without permission
item.description: An artificial intelligence watchdog is accusing OpenAI of training its default ChatGPT model on copyrighted book content without permission.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CERN scientists release blueprint for the Future Circular Collider
item.description: Top minds at the world’s largest atom smasher have released a blueprint for a much bigger successor that could vastly improve research into the remaining enigmas of physics.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Judge blocks social media age-verification law in Arkansas
item.description: The Arkansas law would require people under 18 to only access certain sites with parental permission.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Django 5.2 release touts automatic model importing
item.description: The latest version of the Python web framework features an automatic model-importing capability in the shell. This release also spells the end of support levels for previous version 5.x releases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Modern Warfare
item.description: The integration of artificial intelligence into military operations has the potential to revolutionize warfare, but it requires strategic leadership and a deliberate culture of innovation.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: NetBox Labs launches drift detection tool to tame configuration chaos
item.description: NetBox Labs this week made available a new product that will help network teams detect and remediate configuration drift across sophisticated network environments before costly service disruptions or unplanned downtime affect services or applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google’s new AI tech may know when your house will burn down
item.description: FireSat is a satellite project designed to detect and track wildfires early, aiming to detect a fire that's the size of a classroom within 20 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CMMC review, multiuse SCIFs on radar of DoD acquisition nominee
item.description: Michael Duffey, the nominee to be USD for acquisition and sustainment, told lawmakers he's sensitive to CMMC compliance challenges for small businesses. The post CMMC review, multiuse SCIFs on radar of DoD acquisition nominee first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
